Criteria: A broadcast warning issued by the President or designated authority indicating an imminent or ongoing major strategic attack (nuclear, conventional, or massive cyber) on the United States.
An immediate warning that an actual enemy attack (missile strike, aerial bombing, or conventional military invasion) has been detected or is imminent. Citizens must take immediate cover.
An attack or severe incident at a nuclear power facility causing a critical breach or an immediate threat of radioactive release.
The detonation of a radiological dispersal device ('dirty bomb') or an intentional release of radioactive material targeting a specific area.
An intentional chemical or biological agent attack targeting population centers or critical infrastructure.
A coordinated hostile strike targeting grid systems, water supplies, or communication nodes resulting in cascading, life-threatening infrastructure failures.
A localized mandate ordering citizens to seal themselves indoors due to airborne contaminants, ongoing nearby military/tactical conflict, or unexploded ordnance.
An urgent directive ordering the immediate clearance of a specific zone directly in the path of incoming hostile actions or expanding CBRN contamination plumes.
